events.text: Margaret Moore, 69-year-old Black female, Never smoker. Lives independently. ECOG performance status 1.
Diagnosed with Adenocarcinoma of the Right upper lobe of lung. Clinical staging: T2aN2M0, AJCC Stage IIIA. ICD-10: C34.11. Stage III non-small cell lung cancer with EGFR L858R mutation.
Pathology specimen received for initial biopsy.
PET/CT performed for baseline staging.
Clinical visit for initial oncology consultation.
NGS report received for molecular profiling.
Initiated neoadjuvant chemotherapy: Carboplatin AUC 5 + Pemetrexed 500 mg/m2 q3w x 4 cycles. Cycle 1. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Initiated neoadjuvant chemotherapy: Carboplatin AUC 5 + Pemetrexed 500 mg/m2 q3w x 4 cycles. Cycle 2. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 2 assessment.
Initiated neoadjuvant chemotherapy: Carboplatin AUC 5 + Pemetrexed 500 mg/m2 q3w x 4 cycles. Cycle 3. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Developed grade 1 diarrhea. Managed with loperamide 4 mg then 2 mg after each loose stool. Stool studies negative.
Initiated neoadjuvant chemotherapy: Carboplatin AUC 5 + Pemetrexed 500 mg/m2 q3w x 4 cycles. Cycle 4. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 4 assessment.
Developed grade 2 rash (acneiform). Treated with doxycycline 100 mg BID and topical clindamycin gel.
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast performed for restaging after neoadjuvant chemotherapy.
Clinical visit for results review after neoadjuvant chemotherapy.
Underwent surgical resection: Right upper lobectomy with mediastinal lymph node dissection. Estimated blood loss 300 mL. Length of stay 7 days. Uncomplicated postoperative course.
Pathology specimen received for surgical pathology.
Clinical visit for postoperative follow-up.
Initiated consolidation immunotherapy: Durvalumab 10 mg/kg q2w x 12 months. Cycle 1. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Developed grade 1 diarrhea. Managed with loperamide 4 mg then 2 mg after each loose stool. Stool studies negative.
Initiated consolidation immunotherapy: Durvalumab 10 mg/kg q2w x 12 months. Cycle 2. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 2 assessment.
Initiated consolidation immunotherapy: Durvalumab 10 mg/kg q2w x 12 months. Cycle 3. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Developed grade 3 neutropenia. Treated with G-CSF (filgrastim 5 mcg/kg daily x 5 days). CBC monitored closely.
Initiated consolidation immunotherapy: Durvalumab 10 mg/kg q2w x 12 months. Cycle 4. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 4 assessment.
Developed grade 1 diarrhea. Managed with loperamide 4 mg then 2 mg after each loose stool. Stool studies negative.
Initiated consolidation immunotherapy: Durvalumab 10 mg/kg q2w x 12 months. Cycle 5. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Initiated consolidation immunotherapy: Durvalumab 10 mg/kg q2w x 12 months. Cycle 6. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 6 assessment.
Initiated consolidation immunotherapy: Durvalumab 10 mg/kg q2w x 12 months. Cycle 7. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Developed grade 2 elevated transaminases. ALT 95 U/L, AST 78 U/L. Treatment held one week, then resumed at reduced dose.
Initiated consolidation immunotherapy: Durvalumab 10 mg/kg q2w x 12 months. Cycle 8. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 8 assessment.
Developed grade 3 neutropenia. Treated with G-CSF (filgrastim 5 mcg/kg daily x 5 days). CBC monitored closely.
Initiated consolidation immunotherapy: Durvalumab 10 mg/kg q2w x 12 months. Cycle 9. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Initiated consolidation immunotherapy: Durvalumab 10 mg/kg q2w x 12 months. Cycle 10. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 10 assessment.
Initiated consolidation immunotherapy: Durvalumab 10 mg/kg q2w x 12 months. Cycle 11. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Initiated consolidation immunotherapy: Durvalumab 10 mg/kg q2w x 12 months. Cycle 12. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 12 assessment.
PET/CT performed for restaging after consolidation immunotherapy.
Clinical visit for results review after consolidation immunotherapy.
PET/CT performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Brain MRI with contrast performed for surveillance imaging.
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.

documents.text:
PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Margaret Moore
MRN: patient_5915b7eaad78
Specimen ID: S2023-79105
Date of Procedure: 2023-09-24
Date of Report: 2023-09-27

SPECIMEN: Right upper lobe of lung, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
Adenocarcinoma, poorly differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 6.7 cm
Margins: Negative (closest margin 0.3 cm)
Lymphovascular invasion: Present
Perineural invasion: Not identified

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
TTF-1: Positive
Napsin-A: Positive
CK7: Positive
CK20: Negative
PD-L1 (22C3): TPS 40%

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Margaret Moore - Right upper lobe of lung" is a tan-white tissue fragment measuring 6.7 x 4.7 x 3.4 cm. The cut surface reveals a friable gray-white mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A5.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a poorly differentiated neoplasm composed of cords and trabeculae of atypical cells with moderate cytoplasm and vesicular chromatin. Mitotic figures are frequent (12 per 10 HPF). No necrosis is identified. The surrounding stroma shows minimal reactive changes.

Electronically signed by: James Park, MD
Department of Pathology

PET/CT REPORT

Patient: Margaret Moore
MRN: patient_5915b7eaad78
Date of Study: 2023-09-24

CLINICAL INDICATION: Adenocarcinoma of the right upper lobe of lung. baseline staging.

COMPARISON: None available.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT performed from skull base to iliac crest following administration of 100 mL Omnipaque 350 IV contrast.

FINDINGS:
LUNGS: Spiculated mass in the right upper lobe of lung measuring 4.6 x 3.7 cm. New finding.

MEDIASTINUM: Paratracheal lymphadenopathy, largest node measuring 1.8 cm in short axis.

LIVER: Homogeneous attenuation. No focal lesions.

PANCREAS: Normal in size and attenuation. No pancreatic mass or ductal dilation.

KIDNEYS: Normal in size bilaterally. No hydronephrosis or renal masses.

PELVIS: No pelvic mass or lymphadenopathy. Bladder unremarkable.

BONES: No suspicious osseous lesions. Degenerative changes of the lumbar spine.

IMPRESSION:
1. Right upper lobe mass consistent with primary lung malignancy, as described.
2. Mediastinal lymphadenopathy, likely nodal metastatic disease.

Electronically signed by: Robert Chang, MD
Department of Radiology

INITIAL CONSULTATION - Thoracic Surgery

Patient: Margaret Moore
MRN: patient_5915b7eaad78
Date: 2023-10-02
Provider: Dr. Richard Okafor

CHIEF COMPLAINT: initial oncology consultation

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Mr./Ms. Moore is a 69-year-old female who presents for initial evaluation of newly diagnosed adenocarcinoma of the right upper lobe of lung, IIIA. Patient presented with symptoms prompting workup. Stage III non-small cell lung cancer with EGFR L858R mutation. Pathology confirmed adenocarcinoma. Molecular testing is pending. Patient reports fatigue and unintentional 10-lb weight loss over 3 months.

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:
- peripheral vascular disease
- chronic kidney disease stage 2
- osteoarthritis

SURGICAL HISTORY:
- Cholecystectomy (age 50)

SOCIAL HISTORY:
- Current smoker (1 ppd)
- Alcohol: Social drinker (2-3 drinks/week)
- Occupation: Construction worker (retired)
- Lives with spouse and adult children

FAMILY HISTORY:
- Father with prostate cancer at age 68. Paternal uncle with lung cancer.

ALLERGIES: Iodine contrast (anaphylaxis)

MEDICATIONS:
- None

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
Positive for: night sweats, intermittent nausea, unintentional weight loss, abdominal bloating. Remainder of 14-point ROS reviewed and negative.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Vitals: BP 142/66, HR 90, RR 15, Temp 97.7F, SpO2 97% on RA, Wt 80.1 kg
General: Comfortable, thin-appearing, no acute distress.
HEENT: Normocephalic, atraumatic. Mucous membranes moist.
Neck: No thyromegaly. No lymphadenopathy.
Lungs: Clear bilaterally. No rales or rhonchi.
CV: RRR, S1/S2 normal. No murmurs, gallops, or rubs.
Abdomen: Soft, mildly tender in RUQ. No rebound. Normoactive bowel sounds.
Extremities: Trace bilateral lower extremity edema. Warm and well-perfused.
Neuro: A&O x3, CN II-XII intact, no focal deficits.

LABORATORY RESULTS:
WBC 4.0, Hgb 9.7, Plt 242, Cr 1.03

IMAGING:
See separate imaging report.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
69-year-old female with IIIA adenocarcinoma of the right upper lobe of lung. initial oncology consultation.

1. Adenocarcinoma of the right upper lobe of lung, Stage IIIA
   - Reviewed pathology and imaging with patient and family
   - Recommend neoadjuvant chemotherapy
   - Will discuss at multidisciplinary tumor board
   - Molecular profiling results pending
2. Supportive care
   - Nutrition consultation placed
   - Social work referral for psychosocial support
   - Peripheral access adequate
3. Follow-up in 1-2 weeks to initiate treatment

Electronically signed by: Amanda Foster, MD
Thoracic Surgery

NEXT GENERATION SEQUENCING REPORT

Patient: Margaret Moore
MRN: patient_5915b7eaad78
Date of Report: 2023-10-11
Specimen: Right upper lobe of lung (Resection)
Diagnosis: Adenocarcinoma
Panel: FoundationOne CDx (324 genes)

RESULTS SUMMARY:
Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB): 3.2 mutations/Mb (Low)
Microsatellite Status: MSS

CLINICALLY SIGNIFICANT GENOMIC ALTERATIONS:
  EGFR p.L858R (c.2573T>G) - Missense (Exon 21) [Clinically Significant]

ADDITIONAL PATHOGENIC ALTERATIONS:
  TP53 p.R248W - Missense (Exon 7)
  RB1 Loss - Deletion

VARIANTS OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE:
  ATM p.V2424G - VUS
  BRCA2 p.K1003E - VUS

COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS:
  No significant copy number alterations detected

FUSIONS:
  No ALK, ROS1, RET, or NTRK fusions detected.

THERAPY ASSOCIATIONS:
  - EGFR L858R: FDA-approved targeted therapies include osimertinib (preferred), erlotinib, afatinib, gefitinib.
  - PD-L1 expression should be assessed for immunotherapy eligibility.

Note: This test was performed using hybrid capture-based next generation sequencing.
Sequencing depth: mean 686x coverage.
Tumor content: 29%.

Electronically signed by: Maria Santos, MD, PhD
Molecular Pathology Laboratory

General: Comfortable, thin-appearing, no acute distress.
HEENT: No scleral icterus. Oropharynx clear.
Neck: Supple, no JVD, no cervical lymphadenopathy.
Lungs: Clear to auscultation bilaterally.
CV: RRR, S1/S2 normal. No murmurs, gallops, or rubs.
Abdomen: Soft, non-distended. Well-healed midline surgical scar.
Extremities: No edema. No cyanosis or clubbing.
Neuro: Alert and oriented. No focal neurological deficits.

LABORATORY RESULTS:
WBC 4.1, Hgb 13.2, Plt 225, Cr 0.92

IMAGING:
Most recent imaging reviewed.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
69-year-old female with IIIA adenocarcinoma of the right upper lobe of lung. cycle 2 assessment.

1. Adenocarcinoma - cycle 2 assessment
   - Proceed with next cycle as planned
   - Tumor markers trending favorably
2. Symptom management
   - Neuropathy being monitored
3. Follow-up in 3 weeks

MRN: patient_5915b7eaad78

CLINICAL INDICATION: Adenocarcinoma of the right upper lobe of lung. restaging after neoadjuvant chemotherapy.

COMPARISON: None available.

LIVER: Homogeneous attenuation. No focal lesions.

PANCREAS: Normal in size and attenuation. No pancreatic mass or ductal dilation.

KIDNEYS: Normal in size bilaterally. No hydronephrosis or renal masses.

PELVIS: No pelvic mass or lymphadenopathy. Bladder unremarkable.

BONES: No suspicious osseous lesions. Degenerative changes of the lumbar spine.

IMPRESSION:
1. Right upper lobe mass consistent with primary lung malignancy, decreased.
2. Mediastinal lymphadenopathy, likely nodal metastatic disease.

PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Margaret Moore
MRN: patient_5915b7eaad78
Specimen ID: S2024-58853
Date of Procedure: 2024-02-05
Date of Report: 2024-02-09

SPECIMEN: Right upper lobe of lung, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
Adenocarcinoma, moderately differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 5.1 cm
Margins: Negative (closest margin 0.5 cm)
Lymphovascular invasion: Not identified
Perineural invasion: Present

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
TTF-1: Positive
Napsin-A: Positive
CK7: Positive
CK20: Negative
PD-L1 (22C3): TPS 40%

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Margaret Moore - Right upper lobe of lung" is a tan-white tissue fragment measuring 5.1 x 3.6 x 2.5 cm. The cut surface reveals a soft white mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A9.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a moderately differentiated neoplasm composed of glands of atypical cells with abundant cytoplasm and vesicular chromatin. Mitotic figures are occasionally seen (3 per 10 HPF). Necrosis is present. The surrounding stroma shows desmoplastic reaction.

Electronically signed by: James Park, MD
Department of Pathology

PET/CT REPORT

Patient: Margaret Moore
MRN: patient_5915b7eaad78
Date of Study: 2024-09-05

CLINICAL INDICATION: Adenocarcinoma of the right upper lobe of lung. restaging after consolidation immunotherapy.

COMPARISON: None available.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT performed from skull base to iliac crest following administration of 100 mL Omnipaque 350 IV contrast.

FINDINGS:
LUNGS: Irregular mass in the right upper lobe of lung measuring 2.9 x 2.3 cm. Decreased in size compared to prior.

MEDIASTINUM: Paratracheal lymphadenopathy, largest node measuring 1.9 cm in short axis.

LIVER: Homogeneous attenuation. No focal lesions.

PANCREAS: Normal in size and attenuation. No pancreatic mass or ductal dilation.

KIDNEYS: Normal in size bilaterally. No hydronephrosis or renal masses.

PELVIS: No pelvic mass or lymphadenopathy. Bladder unremarkable.

BONES: No suspicious osseous lesions. Degenerative changes of the lumbar spine.

IMPRESSION:
1. Right upper lobe mass consistent with primary lung malignancy, decreased.
2. Mediastinal lymphadenopathy, likely nodal metastatic disease.
